Selected work
Four roles. One through-line.
Metals & Mining · KPMG
Delivered ₹330 Cr in commercial value across a ₹7,050 Cr capital expansion.
Situation
A leading metals producer was executing one of its largest-ever capital expansions (₹7,050 Cr). With 114 active contracts running simultaneously, the client faced severe financial exposure from disputed claims, cost overruns, and unresolved commercial positions.
Task
Advise senior leadership on which commercial positions to close, contest, or renegotiate under intense time pressure and with real liquidity implications.
Action
  • Modelled the Risk: Performed risk-purchase modelling to quantify the exact cost of contesting versus settling each position.
  • Built the Defence: Conducted claims defence analysis and built targeted recovery strategies for high-exposure contracts.
  • Prioritised Interventions: Worked directly with client leadership to sequence interventions based purely on financial impact.
Result
  • Captured ₹330 Cr in direct value creation.
  • Recommendations directly shaped the client's liquidity prioritisation strategy across the entire expansion portfolio.
Learning
Large capital projects don't fail on strategy. They bleed on contracts. The biggest lever in a ₹7,000 Cr programme isn't the master plan. It's who is managing the commercial exposure underneath it.
Government Infrastructure · Andhra Pradesh
Built the monitoring engine for a ₹5.56 Lakh Cr state infrastructure portfolio.
Situation
The Chief Secretary required real-time visibility across 153 stalled or delayed mega-projects spanning power, ports, roads, and urban infrastructure. The portfolio was massive, the data was heavily fragmented across departments, and intervention priorities were entirely unclear.
Task
Design the analytical backbone and reporting mechanisms for a CM-mandated PMU, giving the executive team a single source of truth across the entire portfolio.
Action
  • Built the Framework: Designed the project monitoring structure and KPI architecture, consolidating fragmented departmental data into a unified reporting layer across all 153 projects.
  • Quantified the Leakage: Built fiscal opportunity-cost frameworks to calculate revenue leakage from delayed capex, converting abstract project delays into hard rupee terms for executive decision making.
  • Delivered the Engine: Deployed the dashboard and reporting mechanisms to give the executive team real-time visibility and clear intervention priorities.
Result
  • Delivered a live monitoring system governing ₹5.56 Lakh Cr in state infrastructure commitments.
  • Directly shaped intervention priorities across the highest-impact stalled projects in the portfolio.
Learning
Government projects don't stall because of bad intent. They stall because no one has converted delay into a number a Chief Secretary can act on. The moment you put a rupee figure on inaction, decisions happen faster.
EdTech · School Wizard
Scaled an EdTech startup from zero to 10,000 users with a 20% paid conversion rate.
Situation
School Wizard was a seed-stage EdTech startup attempting to establish product-market fit during COVID lockdowns. The market was dominated by venture-backed giants like Byju's. The startup faced a critical choice: out-spend the giants (impossible) or out-maneuver them in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities where the big players hadn't built a physical footprint.
Task
Work directly with the founder to design a resource-efficient GTM strategy from scratch, building user acquisition, institutional partnerships, and a monetisation pipeline without a capital war chest.
Action
  • Asymmetric Positioning: Bypassed expensive digital acquisition channels entirely, focusing on a grassroots approach in Tier 2/3 markets through vernacular content and localised offline initiatives.
  • Designed the Model: Developed a scholarship-led monetisation strategy that removed the upfront price barrier for price-sensitive parents while creating a structured free-to-paid conversion pathway.
  • Built Institutional Depth: Directly pitched and secured 100+ school partnerships, turning local school administrations into zero-cost, high-trust acquisition funnels.
Result
  • Grew the platform from zero to 10,000 users within 12 months.
  • Achieved a 20% free-to-paid conversion rate within three months of launch.
  • Delivered 250% user growth during lockdowns by unlocking previously inaccessible rural markets.
Learning
When you don't have a war chest, you cannot play the incumbent's game. We built a B2B2C framework that acquired entire classrooms at zero cost. In low-trust markets, localised distribution and institutional alignment beat a multi-million dollar ad budget every single time.
Field Engineering · ULCCS
Delivered a 44 km highway package 8% under budget while digitising site operations from scratch.
Situation
A major national highway project required strict cost controls across a 44 km structure package. Execution was under simultaneous pressure from severe right-of-way bottlenecks, fragmented equipment tracking, and zero digital visibility into site operations.
Task
Manage on-ground execution, resolve land acquisition blockages, and build the operational visibility infrastructure needed to keep the package on time and under budget.
Action
  • Unlocked the Bottlenecks: Directly negotiated with landowners and district authorities to secure 70+ land approvals, completely eliminating right-of-way blockers.
  • Built Visibility from Zero: Designed and deployed digital reporting and equipment tracking systems from scratch, giving site leadership real-time data on machinery utilisation and cost burn.
  • Controlled the Numbers: Used the new tracking infrastructure to identify underutilised equipment early, optimise workforce deployment, and enforce strict on-ground cost controls.
Result
  • Delivered the structure package 8% under budget.
  • Improved machinery utilisation by 12% and significantly accelerated execution visibility.
  • Secured 70+ land approvals, restoring execution continuity and keeping the project on schedule.
Learning
On a construction site, the biggest strategic risk isn't a bad plan. It's the gap between what the project manager thinks is happening and what is actually happening on the ground. Digital visibility doesn't just improve efficiency. It changes the quality of every decision made above it.
Things I've built
Strategy without execution is just theory.
Government Tender Intelligence Agent
Make.comGroq APILLaMA 3.3 70Beprocure.gov.inGmail
The Problem
29,000+ tenders published daily across India. Finding consultancy and advisory opportunities buried in civil construction noise was taking hours of manual effort every week and still missing relevant bids.
The Solution
A fully automated daily pipeline. A scheduled trigger pulls tenders from central government portals, an AI layer filters strictly for consultancy and advisory opportunities above ₹1 Cr, and a formatted digest lands in the team inbox every morning with zero manual input required.
Shifted the team from searching to deciding. Covers CPPP, GeM, NHAI, ministry portals, and state PWDs simultaneously.
Chief Secretary PMO Dashboard
PythonChart.jsAdvanced ExcelJSON
The Problem
154 infrastructure projects worth ₹3+ lakh crore across Andhra Pradesh tracked across fragmented departmental reports. No unified view, no cross-filtering, and no way to surface intervention priorities quickly for executive decision making.
The Solution
A fully interactive monitoring dashboard with seven cross-linked filters — sector, department, implementation stage, mode, district, and open issues. KPI scorecards, cost vs. time overrun analysis, district investment heatmap, and an auto-generated insights panel in a single view.
Gave the Chief Secretary's office real-time portfolio visibility across the entire state infrastructure programme. Intervention priorities surfaced immediately.
Site Equipment Tracking System
Advanced ExcelPivot ChartsDynamic Formulas
The Problem
Machinery utilisation was invisible on a 44 km highway package. Site engineers booked equipment verbally, the office team had no real-time view of allocation or cost burn, and underutilised assets were bleeding the budget silently.
The Solution
An Excel-based command centre built from scratch. 8 KPI cards, 6 live charts covering utilisation, weekly trend, status breakdown, cost by machine, shift distribution, and daily burn — plus a full booking log with color-coded status tracking across all 12 machines.
12% improvement in machinery utilisation. A key lever in delivering the package 8% under budget.
